content:

Another interesting thing is that only the newer version is in my Programs an Features. It says it was downloaded today. I downloaded a few days ago. I did do a System Restore to take care of another problem. 

I was hoping to uninstall one of them but only the newer version is the one showing in my Programs. I am afraid to uninstall it and end up not having a Firefox at all or to loose all of my Saved Password Logins from the older version that is not showing in my Programs. I have a lot and I use them constantly. Just easy to sign in. Too many different passwords, impossible to remember. I do have a book with all of them but it takes forever to find them. How can I merge all these Saved Passwords Logins to the newer version?

I would really appreciate all the help I can get, please!!!!

Amy

title: DNS over HTTPS at Max Protection Prevents Websites From Loading
content:
When I opened up Firefox this evening, websites stopped working if "DNS over HTTPS" is set to "Max Protection". In some instances, such as YouTube, the website loads, but it acts like you're offline and tells me to turn on my Wi-Fi even though it's already on and running fine. Weirdly, it seems that setting DNS over HTTPS to Max Protection, loading a website, set DNS over HTTPS to a lower setting, and then reloading the website fixes the issue, but it'd still be nice if we didn't have to go through the hassle at all. Thank you for your time and patience.

Sincerely, Watching Forests
